rationale: CRITICAL REGRESSION on evals 002 and 003 (both regression_critical). The baseLayer: false pattern (H2) was adopted by the model but caused non-default imagery layers (OSM, ArcGIS) to not render — all affected evals show satellite imagery instead of the expected cartographic tiles. Root cause: generated code wraps in its own (async () => {})() IIFE which nests inside the template's IIFE, likely creating a timing issue where the viewer is created but imagery layers added asynchronously don't resolve before screenshot. The navigation tooltip fix (H1: navigationHelpButton: false) DID work correctly — eval-001 shows clean viewport. But H2 poisoned the result. Auto-discard per regression policy.

critical_regressions.description: Shows satellite imagery instead of OSM cartography over Paris
Shows satellite imagery + grid instead of OSM + grid over London
Shows satellite imagery instead of ArcGIS street map over DC

hypotheses_validated.H1_navigation_tooltip: VALIDATED — navigationHelpButton: false works correctly, viewport is clean
hypotheses_validated.H2_baseLayer_false: INVALIDATED — caused critical regression. The model adopted baseLayer: false but imagery replacement fails. Likely interaction with eval template's async IIFE wrapping.

next_iteration_notes: Keep H1 (nav tooltip fix). REVERT H2 (baseLayer: false). Keep only the remove+add pattern for base layer swapping. The generated code's own (async ()=>{})() wrapper may be interfering with the template. Investigate whether to instruct the model NOT to wrap code in an async IIFE since the template already provides one.
